originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
content: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

originalTitle: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
content: Judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials.

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
content: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

originalTitle: Union Berlin’s Marie-Louise Eta becomes first woman manager of men’s team
title: Union Berlin’s Marie-Louise Eta becomes first woman manager of men’s team
content: Berlin's Eta becomes first female head coach of a top-tier European club after her appointment by the Bundesliga side.

originalTitle: Saudi Arabia says key oil pipeline back to full capacity after attacks
title: Saudi Arabia says key oil pipeline back to full capacity after attacks
content: The Ministry of Energy says the East-West pipeline is back to pumping about 7 million barrels per day.

originalTitle: Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
title: Legendary Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
content: Bhosle recorded more than 12,000 songs in multiple Indian languages over a seven-decade career.

originalTitle: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
title: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
content: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures point to a record turnout of voters.

originalTitle: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
title: Asha Bhosle: The sound of Bollywood dies aged 92
content: Her infectious voice got fans dancing and singing, becoming the soundtrack for generations of Indians.

originalTitle: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
title: News from Far Away: Let's All Be Friends!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about something happening far, far away. Today, we are talking about two big countries called Ukraine and Russia. They are neighbors, just like your house might be next to your friend's house. But right now, these two neighbors are having a big disagreement. It's like when you and your friend have a little argument, but much, much bigger.

Countries with a Disagreement

Ukraine and Russia are both very large countries in a part of the world called Europe. They have many people, beautiful cities, and amazing nature. But sometimes, even big countries can have big problems being friends. Right now, they are saying unkind things about each other. Each country says the *other* one did something wrong. It's like when you and your sibling both say, "No, *you* started it!" Did you know? Ukraine is famous for its wide-open fields of sunflowers! Sunflowers always face the sun and follow it across the sky. Did you know? Russia is the biggest country in the whole wide world! It's so big that it crosses two continents, Europe and Asia! Imagine a country that takes up so much space on the map!

What is a 'Ceasefire'?

Let's learn a big new word: "Ceasefire." It sounds fancy, but it just means "stop fighting." Imagine you and your friends are playing, and suddenly you start to argue loudly. A ceasefire is like when an adult says, "Okay, everyone, calm down! Let's stop arguing for a bit and try to be nice." These two countries, Ukraine and Russia, made a promise a while ago to stop their disagreements and not fight. They agreed to have a ceasefire. It was a promise to be quiet and try to be peaceful for a while. Did you know? The word "ceasefire" literally means to stop shooting guns or fighting. It's all about making a promise to be peaceful for a short time. Did you know? When people agree to a ceasefire, they hope it will help them talk and solve their problems without any more anger or loud arguments. They want to find a way to make peace.

When Promises Are Broken

Now, the news says that both Ukraine and Russia are saying the *other* one broke the ceasefire promise. They are saying the other country did things they promised not to do. This is called a "violation" – when someone does not keep their word or breaks an agreement. They are saying these promises were broken hundreds of times! That's a lot of broken promises, isn't it? It's like if you promised not to eat all the cookies before dinner, but then your friend says you did, and you say *they* did, over and over again! When promises are broken, it makes it very hard for people to trust each other again. It makes it much harder to be good friends and neighbors. Did you know? Trust is like a special glue that holds friendships together. When promises are broken, the glue gets weak and the friendship can feel wobbly.

Ukraine's Leader Speaks

Every country has a leader, just like your classroom has a teacher or your school has a head teacher. The leader of Ukraine is called President Volodymyr Zelensky. President Zelensky said something important. He said that if Russia bothers his country, Ukraine's soldiers will respond. It's like saying, "If you push my ball away, I will push yours away too!" He wants to show that Ukraine will stand strong and not let anyone push them around. We all hope that instead of pushing or getting angry, they can find a way to talk calmly and kindly. Talking is always better than pushing or fighting, don't you think? Did you know? Presidents are very busy people! They work hard every day to make good choices for all the children and adults in their country, trying to keep everyone safe and happy.

Why Being Friends is Best

When countries have big disagreements and break promises, it makes many, many people sad. Children can't always play outside safely. Sometimes, homes can get damaged, and people have to leave their towns to find a safe place. It is much, much better when everyone is kind and tries to talk things out. When people are friends, they can help each other, share happy times, and build wonderful things together, like schools and hospitals. Did you know? When countries are friends, they can share ideas, trade yummy food, and even learn new songs and games from each other! Friendship makes the world a richer place.

What Can We Learn?

Even though this news is from very far away, it reminds us of something very important: always try to be a good friend and a good neighbor in your own life. If you have a disagreement with a friend, try to talk it out with kind words. Listen to what your friend has to say, even if you don't agree right away. Sharing your feelings and really listening to others can solve almost any problem. It's okay to feel sad or angry sometimes, but it's always best to use your words, not your hands or angry shouts. Remember to be gentle and patient. Did you know? Talking things through is like a magic key that can open the door to solving problems and making friendships even stronger! It helps everyone understand each other.

Let's Hope for Peace

We hope that Ukraine and Russia can stop their disagreement very soon. We wish they can learn to be good neighbors and find peace. Everyone in the world wants to live in peace, with smiles and happy days, not frowns and arguments. Sending big wishes for peace from Africa to Ukraine and Russia! Did you know? A beautiful white dove bird is a special symbol of peace all around the world! It reminds us to hope for calm and quiet. Did you know? Many, many children just like you, all over the planet, wish for peace every single day. Let's all wish for it together and be peacemakers in our own communities! That's all for now, my lovely friends. Stay kind and keep shining!
summary: Two countries far away, Ukraine and Russia, are having a big disagreement and saying each other broke promises to stop fighting, making everyone hope they can talk and find peace instead.
whyMatters: This news shows us how important it is for everyone, even big countries, to be good friends, talk out problems, and keep promises so that everyone can live safely and happily without fighting.

content: Nine points clear with six games to play - could Arsenal still really blow the Premier League title from that position?

originalTitle: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
title: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
content: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.
